posture: Quiet Sunday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a-§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Today is a Sunday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. Several recurring or undated items were again considered and excluded: the NSW commercial FOGO mandate (business-tier obligations commenced 1 July) is a regulatory-rollout milestone, not a fresh innovation signal; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial and its associated FOGO deferral resurfaced only via older (November 2025 / January 2026) coverage; the Council of Mayors (SEQ) $4m multi-unit food-waste diversion pilot dates to mid-June and is out-of-window; the NSW AI-in-planning tools (Early Adopter Grant Program, DAISY, AI Solutions Panel) produced no new dated development; and overseas material (Bloomberg 2025-26 Mayors Challenge winners, UK Barnet AI-assisted planning prototype, Luján de Cuyo AI pothole detection) was either out-of-window, an awards/programme notice rather than a fresh transferable council signal, or general policy. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
